supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on Amazon Web Services and other infrastructure-as-a-service providers for cloud-based products; Dependency on TD Synnex as largest distributor (33% of net revenue in fiscal 2025); Reliance on third-party developers for complementary products
supply_chain.risks: Loss of or significant reduction in business with TD Synnex could harm business; Failure of third-party service providers could result in loss of data or service disruptions; Supply chain attacks and cybersecurity incidents; Reliance on single or limited number of suppliers in certain countries

regulatory_risks: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in European Union - penalties up to €20 million or 4% of annual global revenue
Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL) in China - penalties up to 50 million renminbi or 5% of annual revenue
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA) - penalties up to $7,500 per violation
New state privacy laws in multiple U.S. states with varying requirements
ePrivacy Regulation in EU and UK with increased fines for non-compliance
AI Act in European Union imposing obligations on AI technology providers
U.S. state laws addressing AI development and use
Export controls and economic sanctions laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, U.K. Bribery Act)
Cross-border data transfer restrictions and data localization requirements in China, India, Australia, New Zealand, Brazil, Japan
FedRAMP certification requirements for U.S. federal government contracts
Digital services taxes and regulations by foreign governments
Changes in tax laws including Inflation Reduction Act (15% corporate alternative minimum tax)
OECD proposals for global minimum tax rate of 15%
Cookie and tracking technology regulations in EU and UK
Government procurement regulations and compliance requirements
Intellectual property laws varying by jurisdiction
Open source software license compliance requirements
Section 404 of Sarbanes-Oxley Act internal control requirements

sanction_risks: U.S. and global sanctions related to Russia-Ukraine war
Potential retaliatory sanctions from affected countries
Heightened risk of cyberattacks from nation-state actors including Russian actors
U.S.-China trade tensions and potential tariffs
Trade policy changes including termination of trade agreements and imposition of tariffs
Retaliatory actions by foreign governments including China
Digital services tax disputes and potential U.S. retaliation
Trends toward nationalism and protectionism weakening international trade pacts
